stem: For a positive parameter \( t \), let \( A(t) \) be the area of the region under the curve \( y = [[k]]x^{2} \) and above the \( x \)-axis from \( x = 0 \) to \( x = t \). Find \( A(t) \) when \( t = [[b]] \).
distractor_rationales: This integrates \( [[k]]x \) instead of \( [[k]]x^{2} \).
This forgets to divide by 3 when integrating \( x^{2} \).
This squares the parameter \( [[k]] \) in the coefficient.
This drops the coefficient \( [[k]] \) entirely.
This is off by \( [[k]] \) from the correct area.

stem: For a positive parameter \( t \), let \( V(t) \) be the volume of the solid formed by revolving about the \( x \)-axis the region under the line \( y = [[k]]x \) from \( x = 0 \) to \( x = t \). Find \( V(t) \) when \( t = [[b]] \).
distractor_rationales: This drops the factor of \( \pi \) entirely.
This forgets to divide by 3, using \( b^{2} \) instead of \( b^{3}/3 \).
This drops the square on the coefficient \( [[k]] \).
This doubles the correct volume.
This is off by \( \pi \) from the correct volume.

stem: For a positive parameter \( t \), let \( A(t) \) be the area of the region under the curve \( y = 3\cdot[[k]]x^{2} \) and above the \( x \)-axis from \( x=0 \) to \( x=t \), so that \( A(t) = [[k]]t^{3} \). For what value of \( t \) does \( A(t) = [[M]] \)?
distractor_rationales: This forgets to take a cube root, leaving \( t^{3} \) instead of \( t \).
This takes a square root instead of a cube root.
This takes the cube root of \( M \) alone, without first dividing by \( k \).
This is off by one from the correct value of \( t \).
This is off by one from the correct value of \( t \).
This triples the correct value of \( t \).

stem: For a parameter \( t \) with \( 0 \le t \le [[k]] \), let \( A(t) \) be the area of the region between the line \( y = [[k]]x \) and the parabola \( y = x^{2} \), from \( x = 0 \) to \( x = t \). Find \( A(t) \) when \( t = [[b]] \).
solution: \( A(t) = \int_{0}^{t}\left([[k]]x - x^{2}\right)dx = \dfrac{[[k]]t^{2}}{2} - \dfrac{t^{3}}{3} \). At \( t = [[b]] \) this gives \( A([[b]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This drops the \( -x^{2} \) term of the integrand.
This drops the \( [[k]]x \) term of the integrand.
This evaluates the integrand at \( x=[[b]] \) instead of integrating it.
This is off by \( [[k]] \) from the correct area.
This is off by \( [[k]] \) from the correct area.
This doubles the correct area.
